questions.question: When you buy a bond, you are:
questions.explanation: A bond is a debt instrument — you are lending money to the issuer (company or government) in exchange for regular interest payments and return of principal.

questions.question: In a company bankruptcy, who gets paid first?
questions.explanation: Bondholders (creditors) are paid before any stockholders. The order is: bondholders → preferred stockholders → common stockholders.

questions.question: Which type of bond is considered the safest (risk-free benchmark)?
questions.explanation: US Treasury bonds are backed by the full faith and credit of the US government and are considered the risk-free benchmark.

questions.question: The historical average annual return of the S&P 500 is approximately:
questions.explanation: The S&P 500 has historically returned approximately 10% per year on average (before inflation). After inflation, it's roughly 7%.
